headline: GLC Reform Tracker Shows 22% of Targets Met After 2 Years
context: The MAHB, Petronas, and Tenaga-led GLC transformation programme launched in 2023 achieved only 22% of its 180 reform KPIs after 2 years, according to the PCG's 2025 progress report.

cards.big: 22% KPI achievement after 2 years — Malaysia's GLC reform is failing by its own scorecard
cards.sub: GLCs control RM640 billion in assets — 42% of Bursa Malaysia market capitalisation — making reform urgent

cards.big: GLC returns on equity averaged 6.8% in 2025 versus 11.2% for private sector Bursa-listed companies
cards.sub: Underperformance costs EPF and PNB unitholders an estimated RM14 billion annually in foregone returns

cards.big: GLCs employ 480,000 workers but CEO-to-median pay ratio averages 48:1 versus 28:1 in private sector
cards.sub: Political appointees fill 35% of GLC board seats — down from 42% in 2023 but still above 20% target

cards.big: PCG transformation office has 12 staff monitoring reform across 68 GLCs — capacity fundamentally insufficient
cards.sub: Previous GLC transformation under Khazanah 2004-2015 had 85-person dedicated team with ministerial backing

cards.big: Reforming GLCs with a 12-person team overseeing RM640 billion in assets isn't reform — it's performance theatre
Should GLC boards be required to have majority independent directors with zero political party affiliations?
